campbell.title: Campbell City Council — May 5, 2026
campbell.summary: Campbell City Council met to discuss transit authority finances, adopt a new policy limiting city property use for federal civil enforcement, and address job openings and staff retention challenges.
keyTopics: Santa Clara Valley Transportation Authority's March 2026 investment report
New policy prohibiting city property use for federal civil enforcement
City job vacancies and employee retention strategies

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — April 15, 2026
saratoga.summary: The City Council held a public hearing to address concerns about cleaning up hazardous conditions on certain properties identified by the County, and voted to move forward with the abatement work. The Council also adopted a new technology policy for the city and established a process for residents to request accommodations.
keyTopics: Public hearing on hazardous property cleanup and resident objections
County-identified properties subject to abatement orders
New City Council Technology Disruption Policy
Process for residents to request reasonable accommodations

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos City Council — May 5, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos City Council met to approve routine administrative items and address fire safety concerns. Key actions included declaring overgrown vegetation a public nuisance and approving landscape maintenance contracts, plus renewing service agreements with outside vendors.
keyTopics: Declaring hazardous brush and overgrown vegetation a public nuisance
Approving landscape and lighting maintenance reports
Renewing multi-year service agreements with contractors
Approving minutes from previous April meeting

san-jose.title: San José City Council — May 13, 2026
san-jose.summary: The San José City Council held a meeting to prepare for their upcoming May 19 session by reviewing and organizing agenda items, assigning time slots to discussions, and handling administrative planning. The council also addressed appointment decisions for various city committees and boards, and reviewed the meeting schedule for the next fiscal year.
keyTopics: May 19 City Council meeting agenda finalized and organized
Council appointments to committees, boards, and commissions
Fiscal Year 2026-2027 meeting schedule approved
Staff travel requests reviewed

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — May 14, 2026
mountain-view.summary: This was a notice about how Mountain View residents can participate in an upcoming City Council meeting scheduled for May 14, 2026. The city is asking people to submit questions in advance and providing information about where to find the meeting agenda and how to contact city officials.
keyTopics: Submit public questions before the meeting using the online form
Meeting agendas posted at least 24 hours in advance
Contact City Manager's Office at 650-903-6301 or CNC@MountainView.gov with agenda questions

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — May 5, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Sunnyvale City Council held a special closed-door meeting on May 5, 2026, followed by a regular council meeting. The specific matters discussed in the closed session were not publicly disclosed, as is typical for confidential council discussions.
keyTopics: Closed session meeting held (details not publicly shared)
Regular council meeting convened
Small business agenda item

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — May 11, 2026
cupertino.summary: The City Council held an initial review session on the proposed budget for fiscal year 2026-27 to discuss spending plans before final approval. The meeting also covered requirements for lobbyists to register and report their activities if they attempt to influence city decisions.
keyTopics: FY 2026-27 budget review and planning
Lobbyist registration and disclosure requirements under city law
Public testimony opportunities on budget proposals

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — May 14, 2026
santa-clara.summary: The Santa Clara City Council meeting on May 14, 2026 included standard legal disclosures about how residents can challenge city decisions in court. The council outlined that any lawsuits or legal challenges to city decisions must be filed within 90 days after a decision becomes final, following state law.
keyTopics: 90-day deadline for filing lawsuits against city decisions
Legal challenge procedures under California Code of Civil Procedure Section 1094.6

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — May 5, 2026
milpitas.summary: The Milpitas City Council met to approve the city's five-year capital improvement plan (2027-2031) and reviewed the upcoming operating budget for 2026-27. The council also adopted a new master plan for parks and trails in the Innovation District and updated internal procedures for how council meetings are run.
keyTopics: 2027-2031 Capital Improvement Plan approved
FY 2026-27 Operating Budget presentation and direction
Innovation District Parks and Trails Master Plan adopted
Updates to City Council meeting procedures and handbook

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — May 7, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The City Council held a public hearing on a proposal to build a 6-story apartment building with 72 units on a parking lot at 450 Lytton Avenue. All units would be permanently affordable for low-income residents, and the developer is asking for special approvals under state law to make the project feasible on the small lot.
keyTopics: 72 affordable apartments planned for 450 Lytton Avenue
Building would replace 52-space city parking lot
Six-story building on less than half an acre
Request for density bonus waivers under California state law
Environmental review underway to determine if exemption applies
